description: Docker worker target support for pibo debug pty scenarios, including capability detection, PTY execution inside a container, and artifact retrieval.

userStories.title: Add Docker target option handling
userStories.description: As a Ralph agent, I want to target a named Docker worker so that PTY validation runs in my implementation environment.
acceptanceCriteria: --docker-worker <name> selects Docker execution for scenario runs
--workdir <path> sets the command working directory inside the container
The Docker backend validates that the named container exists and is running before scenario execution
Worker name, container id when available, and workdir are recorded in metadata.json
Focused Docker option and validation tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Detect Docker PTY capabilities
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want clear diagnostics for missing container PTY capabilities so that I can choose a fallback or fix the worker image.
acceptanceCriteria: Detect a usable PTY method inside the container, preferring an internal helper and falling back to Python pty, script, or tmux when available
Missing capability errors name the worker, checked tools, and suggested fallback
metadata.json records the selected PTY method
Tests cover capability detection success and missing-capability diagnostics using mocked Docker command output or equivalent
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Execute scenarios inside Docker worker
userStories.description: As a Ralph agent, I want the same scenario semantics in Docker as on host so that validation gates are portable.
acceptanceCriteria: Run scenario command and steps inside the selected worker with PTY semantics
Support waits, typeText, writeBytes, press, sleep, expectations, and rejects through the Docker backend
Wall-clock timeout and idle timeout terminate the container-side process and record diagnostics
A deterministic Docker PTY smoke passes when required worker capabilities are present or is documented as skipped when unavailable
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Make Docker artifacts host-readable
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want Docker-run artifacts available from the host so that I can inspect PTY failures after a Ralph run.
acceptanceCriteria: Docker-run artifacts are copied to or written under the requested host artifact directory
Failure output prints the host-readable artifact path
Artifact metadata identifies Docker target details and PTY method
Tests or documented smoke verify artifact retrieval for a deterministic command
Typecheck passes
